metadata.archetype_justification: Radarr owns persistent state (SQLite/PostgreSQL databases) and exposes full CRUD operations via 164 REST API endpoints with entity lifecycle management.

findings.title: No Machine Identity Authentication
findings.description: Radarr uses a single shared API key for all API authentication. The key is generated from Guid.NewGuid() and stored in an XML config file. All clients share the same key with no principal attribution. No JWT, OAuth client-credentials, mTLS, or service-account support exists.
findings.gap: No machine identity authentication with principal attribution. Cannot distinguish which client made a specific API call.
findings.recommendation: Implement per-client API key issuance with a principal field stored alongside each key. Add principal to request context for audit attribution.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Implement per-client API key issuance with a client_id or principal field stored alongside each key.
blocker_remediation.target_state: Each agent instance has a unique API key with the principal identity recorded in all audit-relevant logs.

findings.title: No Scoped Permissions (Least Privilege)
findings.description: Single API key grants full access to all 164 endpoints — read, write, delete, system restart, backup restore. No mechanism to issue restricted keys.
findings.gap: No least-privilege enforcement. An agent intended for read-only queries has the same access as a system administrator.
findings.recommendation: Introduce role-based API keys (read-only, media-management, admin) mapping to allowed endpoint sets.

compensating_controls: Deploy API gateway or reverse proxy restricting endpoints per agent key.
Scope agent usage to read-only via orchestration-layer policy.

findings.title: No Action-Level Authorization
findings.description: No per-action authorization checks. Once authenticated, any HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) is permitted on any endpoint.
findings.gap: Cannot restrict an agent to read operations while denying delete within the same resource type.
findings.recommendation: Add authorization policies per HTTP method/endpoint group using ASP.NET Core policy-based authorization.

compensating_controls: Limit agent tool definitions to exclude destructive operations.
Deploy reverse proxy with method-level filtering per client.

findings.title: No Immutable Audit Logging
findings.description: No immutable audit logging. Rotating text log files are mutable. No principal attribution in HTTP logs. No CloudTrail or append-only storage.
findings.gap: Cannot attribute API actions to specific callers. Log files are not immutable or tamper-evident.
findings.recommendation: Add principal identity to HTTP logging middleware. Forward structured logs to an immutable external store.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, audit logging still important for read agents
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write operations but audit logging is still relevant for system maturity and forensics.
compensating_controls: Forward logs to external immutable store (CloudWatch Logs with retention lock).
Deploy API gateway that logs all requests with caller identity.

findings.title: No Agent Identity Suspension Mechanism
findings.description: No mechanism to suspend individual agent identities. Revoking the single shared API key disables all API access.
findings.gap: Cannot isolate a misbehaving agent without taking down all API consumers.
findings.recommendation: Implement per-client keys with active/suspended status. Provide admin endpoint to toggle key status.

compensating_controls: Deploy API gateway with per-client keys that can be individually revoked.
Use network-level controls (IP allowlisting) to isolate agent access.

findings.title: No Inbound API Rate Limiting
findings.description: No inbound API rate limiting middleware. Only outbound rate limiting for external service calls. A runaway agent loop could submit unlimited requests per second.
findings.gap: No protection against agent traffic storms overwhelming the application.
findings.recommendation: Add ASP.NET Core rate limiting middleware (Microsoft.AspNetCore.RateLimiting) with per-client sliding window policies.

compensating_controls: Deploy Radarr behind reverse proxy with rate limiting per client.
Implement agent-side rate limiting in tool orchestration layer.

findings.title: Sensitive Credentials Exposed in API Responses
findings.description: HostConfigResource endpoint returns API key, password hash, SSL cert password, proxy credentials, and PostgreSQL credentials. Provider settings include passwords serialized in responses.
findings.gap: Agent-facing API responses include sensitive credentials. HostConfig endpoint returns system API key and password hash.
findings.recommendation: Add [JsonIgnore] to sensitive fields in HostConfigResource. Create separate credential management endpoints excluded from agent tool definitions.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, B1 fires as RISK-SAFETY since sensitive fields returned in API
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: DATA-Q1 B1 fired because agent-facing APIs return credentials (API key, password hash, proxy passwords) in HostConfigResource GET response.

b1.reasoning: agent_scope is read-only AND sensitive fields (API key, password hash, proxy credentials) are returned in GET /api/v3/config/host response.

b2.reasoning: No access control differentiation — single API key grants access to all data including credentials.

b3.reasoning: No formal classification metadata. PrivacyLevel annotations exist but do not prevent serialization.

findings.title: Potential PII Leakage in Trace-Level Logs
findings.description: Comprehensive log cleansing for secrets (20+ regex patterns). However, trace-level logging can capture request/response bodies. Regex-based approach may miss new patterns.
findings.gap: Trace-level body logging could capture user-submitted data. Regex scrubbing is not guaranteed complete for all patterns.
findings.recommendation: Audit trace-level logging for user content. Consider structured log field allowlist rather than regex-only scrubbing.

compensating_controls: Keep log level at Info in production — trace body logging is disabled by default.
Restrict log file access to the Radarr admin user only.

findings.title: OpenAPI Spec Exists but Lacks Enrichment
findings.description: OpenAPI 3.0.4 specification exists, auto-generated via Swashbuckle. Many response schemas lack detailed descriptions and example values.
findings.gap: Spec exists but response schemas lack descriptions and examples for agent tool generation quality.
findings.recommendation: Enrich OpenAPI spec with response examples and field descriptions for key endpoints.

findings.title: Error Responses Lack Machine-Readable Codes
findings.description: Structured JSON error bodies with Message, Description, Content. No retryable flag or error code enum. Agents cannot distinguish retriable from terminal errors without parsing messages.
findings.gap: No machine-readable error code or retryable indicator.
findings.recommendation: Add errorCode enum and retryable boolean to ErrorModel.

findings.title: Primary Movie Endpoint Lacks Pagination
findings.description: Pagination on some endpoints (blocklist, queue, history) but the primary GET /api/v3/movie returns ALL movies with no pagination.
findings.gap: Primary entity endpoint has no pagination — unbounded result sets for large libraries.
findings.recommendation: Add pagination to GET /api/v3/movie. Add sparse fieldset support.

findings.title: No Automated Breaking-Change Detection
findings.description: API versioned at URL level (/api/v3/). No breaking-change detection in CI. No consumer-driven contract tests. No deprecation notices.
findings.gap: No automated breaking-change detection in CI pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI spec diff (oasdiff) as CI step. Publish API CHANGELOG.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing
findings.description: NLog-based logging with consistent format and CLEF support. HTTP middleware sequence IDs. No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, or traceparent propagation. Not JSON-structured by default.
findings.gap: No distributed tracing. No correlation IDs across request lifecycle.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenTelemetry SDK with ASP.NET Core instrumentation. Switch to JSON log format with traceId.

findings.title: No Error Rate or Latency Alerting
findings.description: Sentry for error reporting. Built-in health checks. No alerting thresholds for API error rates or latency.
findings.gap: No automated alerting on API degradation.
findings.recommendation: Expose Prometheus metrics endpoint. Configure alerting for agent-accessible deployments.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No IaC in repository. Self-hosted application relying on user-managed infrastructure. No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, or Helm charts.
findings.gap: No IaC-defined integration surface for agent-accessible deployments.
findings.recommendation: Create reference IaC templates for agent-accessible deployment patterns (Docker Compose with reverse proxy, Helm chart).

findings.title: No API Contract Testing in CI
findings.description: Comprehensive CI/CD pipeline (Azure Pipelines + GitHub Actions) with unit, integration, automation tests. No API contract testing or breaking-change detection.
findings.gap: API changes not validated against a contract in CI.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI spec diff step in CI. Consider Pact tests for key endpoints.

findings.title: No Automated Rollback Capability
findings.description: Self-updater and versioned packages exist. No automated rollback. Forward-only database migrations. Manual rollback requires reinstallation.
findings.gap: No fast automated rollback if release breaks agent-facing APIs.
findings.recommendation: Add down-migration support. Document rollback procedures. Consider feature flags.

findings.title: No Sandbox Environment for Agent Testing
findings.description: Dev container exists. CI tests against multiple databases. No production-equivalent sandbox with seed data for agent testing.
findings.gap: No sandbox environment with production-equivalent data shape for agent integration testing.
findings.recommendation: Create Docker Compose environment with seed data for agent integration testing.

evaluations.reason: Extended question not triggered. Trigger: Service has operations >30s OR long-running workflows.
Extended question not triggered. Trigger: Service has state changes.
Extended question not triggered. Archetype-calibrated to INFO for stateful-crud with single-user model.
Extended question not triggered. Trigger: Service has external dependencies.
Extended question not triggered. Trigger: Service is P0 priority OR on critical path.
